old_claim.note: Video Endoscopy Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Modern laparoscopic surgery requires an endoscopic visual system so surgeons can operate through small ports while viewing the operative field.

source_shape.source_locator: JSLS history article sections on basic laparoscopic cholecystectomy instruments and early procedures.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The article identifies the laparoscope as one of the three most important basic instruments in the first laparoscopic cholecystectomy and describes later spread through endoscopic technology and training.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a required endoscopic-visual-system edge because operating through small ports depends on a laparoscope or equivalent endoscopic video view.

ontology_before: Laparoscopic surgery treated video endoscopy as a generic enabler.
ontology_after: Modern laparoscopic surgery now treats endoscopic visualization as a hard method dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: laparoscopic surgery's endoscopy edge is required.
would_reject_if: The node were re-scoped to non-video diagnostic laparoscopy rather than modern laparoscopic surgery.
A source showed modern laparoscopic operations can be performed without an endoscopic visual system.
The graph removed aseptic surgery context while making visualization required.
short_reason: Laparoscopic surgery requires endoscopic visualization of the operative field.
